title: Cat Walking in Circles to the Left: Causes and Care Guide
meta_desc: Learn why your cat is walking in circles to the left, when to see a vet, and how to identify signs of vestibular disease or neurological issues.
content_html:
TL;DR: Walking in circles usually indicates a vestibular or neurological issue that requires a veterinary exam within 24 hours. Seek immediate emergency care if the circling is accompanied by seizures, loss of consciousness, or extreme lethargy.

What causes my cat to start walking in circles?

When a cat begins walking in persistent circles to one side, it typically indicates a disruption in their vestibular system. This system, which involves the inner ear and specific parts of the brain, is responsible for maintaining balance and orientation. When one side is affected, the cat feels as though they are tilting or falling, leading them to circle toward the side of the lesion.

Is it an emergency if my cat is walking in circles?

  • Urgency Level: This symptom is classified as Medium urgency; while rarely an immediate life-threatening event, it indicates a significant neurological or middle-ear problem.
  • Timeline: Contact your veterinarian or an urgent care clinic to schedule an appointment within the next 24 hours.
  • Emergency Signs: Seek immediate emergency care if the circling is accompanied by seizures, loss of consciousness, or extreme lethargy.

How does a video of my cat circling help with a veterinary diagnosis?

  • Capturing a video is one of the most helpful tools you can provide, as stress at the clinic can temporarily mask or alter behavioral symptoms.
  • A clear video allows the veterinarian to observe the exact diameter of the circle.
  • Visuals help identify the presence of a head tilt or rhythmic eye movements (nystagmus), which help differentiate between an ear infection and a brain-related issue.

Clinical Context (Merck Veterinary Manual)

Lesions in the cerebral cortex and diencephalon can cause compulsive circling. Vestibular dysfunction can cause ipsilateral falling, rolling, or circling. Medical problems must be excluded as the cause of such signs. Myopathies and focal seizures can also present with similar signs.

Chapter: Behavioral Medicine, Dermatology, Neurology

Source: The Merck Veterinary Manual, 11th Edition (Page 1574)

faqs.question: Can an ear infection cause my cat to walk in circles?
faqs.answer: Yes, a severe infection in the middle or inner ear is one of the most common reasons for vestibular signs like circling and head tilting.

faqs.question: Is my cat dizzy when they circle?
faqs.answer: Yes, cats walking in circles are often experiencing a sensation similar to vertigo, which can also cause nausea and a decreased appetite.

faqs.question: Could circling be a sign of a stroke?
faqs.answer: While less common in cats than in humans, vascular events or 'strokes' in the brain can cause sudden neurological changes, including circling to one side.
